---
title: "marquee_color_labels"
---

[< Back to Reference](../reference.qmd) | [View source](https://codeberg.org/usrbinr/stwd/src/branch/main/R/stwd.R#L243)

# `marquee_color_labels()`

Format Labels with Matching Colors

## Usage

```r
marquee_color_labels(labels, colors, bold = FALSE, italic = FALSE)
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `labels` | Character vector of labels to format. |
| `colors` | Character vector of colors (same length as labels). Can be hex codes (e.g., "#FF0000") or named colors (e.g., "red"). |
| `bold` | Logical, make text bold. Default: FALSE. |
| `italic` | Logical, make text italic. Default: FALSE. |

## Value

Character vector of marquee-formatted labels.

## Description

Creates marquee-formatted text labels where the text color matches
a specified fill or color value. Use with `marquee::geom_marquee()`.

## Examples

```r
labels <- c("Positive", "Negative")
colors <- c("#006400", "#8B0000")
marquee_color_labels(labels, colors)
# Returns: c("{#006400 Positive}", "{#8B0000 Negative}")

# With bold formatting:
marquee_color_labels(labels, colors, bold = TRUE)
# Returns: c("{#006400 **Positive**}", "{#8B0000 **Negative**}")
```
